Off Calendar May-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ODDSJAM, INC. AND OPTICODDS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PERSON MOST QUALIFIED AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND SANCTIONS

Appearances:
Jeffrey Fisher and Alex Reese of Farella Braun & Martel LLP (415.954.4400, jfisher@fbm.com, areese@fbm.com) for defendants OddsJam, Inc. and OpticOdds, Inc.
David Kowalski of Latham & Watkins LLP (858.523.5400, david.kowalski@lw.com) for plaintiff Swish Analytics, Inc.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: Plaintiff Swish Analytics, Inc. and defendants OddsJam, Inc. and OpticOdds, Inc. (collectively "OddsJam") bring parallel motions to compel further deposition and production of documents pursuant to their respective PMQ notices. Both PMQ notices covered a broad range of topics and included extensive document requests. The court directed the parties to meet and confer and has conducted two informal discovery conferences with the parties, receiving letter briefing with extensive exhibits in advance of each IDC.

The deposition transcripts of Mr. Restivo and Mr. Skoff reveal not unprepared witnesses but the challenge of tackling such broad topics without the benefit of document production. The court orders both parties to substantially complete production of all documents responsive to the PMQ notice document requests by July 8, 2026. The parties may elect to prioritize certain categories of documents for expedited review and production; the court does not make a prioritization order (except as specified below) but directs the parties to continue to make rolling productions as they complete review.

Much of the discussion during the IDCs has concerned the scope of collection and review obligations, and particularly whether the parties must search for and review for responsiveness all documents that contain any of 36 sportsbook names. Unless the parties reach a different agreement, the court directs both parties to run that search for at least three sportsbook names specified by the opposing party, do a full review of those hits, and produce all responsive documents by the court's substantial completion deadline. For the remaining sportsbook names, the parties can run OddsJam's proposed narrower searches (except for external communications, where they have agreed to run searches for all sportsbooks) and produce based on that narrower collection. Nothing in this order prevents a party from seeking broader collection based on a comparison of the narrow search results to the broad search results.

For remaining items where the parties dispute search terms, reflected in pages 5-7 of OddsJam's May 7, 2026 letter brief as item numbers 2, 3, 7, 8, and 10, the court orders OddsJam to sample a statistically significant number of hits, determine the responsiveness rate of those hits, disclose to Swish the number sampled and the responsiveness rate, and produce the responsive documents. The parties can have a better discussion about the reasonableness of the search terms at that point. This sampling and initial production should occur by June 8, 2026, so that continued discussions and full production occur by the July 8 substantial completion deadline. For items where OddsJam disputes Swish's search terms, the parties will undertake the same procedure just identified using the same timeframes.

The parties' motions for orders compelling further PMQ depositions on the notices at issue here are denied without prejudice to renewal after the substantial completion of document production.

Both parties sought sanctions. The court concludes both sides acted with substantial justification and does not award sanctions.

The court will issue an order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR 10619 (925.384.0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tenative ruling as modified.

PLAINTIFF SWISH ANALYTICS, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Discovery From

Plaintiff's motion to compel jurisdictional discovery is granted in part and continued to July 2, 2025 for further hearing.

On April 18, 2025, after plaintiff filed this motion, defendant produced an additional 1,076 documents that purportedly respond to plaintiff's discovery requests. (Bloodworth Decl., par. 29.) In addition, defendant has represented that more documents will be provided once the parties agree on the terms of a protective order. (Id. at 30.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer forthwith on the terms of the protective order.

In the meantime, the court orders the following:

The court overrules defendants' objection to the lookback period identified by plaintiff. Defendants' production shall relate back to defendants' foundings: OpticOdds (September 2023) and OddsJam (April 2021). (Restivo Decl., par. 3.) The evidence is "likely to lead to the production of evidence of facts establishing jurisdiction" and a lookback period of "several years" prior to the filing of the complaint is reasonable. (Hardell v. Vanzyl (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 960, 975, 973.) Defendants fail to show that such production is unduly burdensome. (W. Pico Furniture Co. of L.A. v. Super. Ct. (1961) 56 Cal.2d 407, 417.)

The court overrules the relevance and overbreadth objections to RFP No. 1. While the relationship among the defendant, the forum, and litigation determines jurisdiction (not plaintiff's or third parties' contacts), intentional targeting can support jurisdiction and the requested information meets the discovery standard. (Walden v. Fiore (2014) 571 U.S. 277.)

The parties will meet and confer about RFP 30 in light of Defendant's further production. The Court continues this issue to July 2, 2025.

The court overrules defendants' objections to producing internal documents (RFPs 30, 36, 39, and 41). Internal documents regarding defendants' California contacts and its ambitions are relevant to intentional targeting and purposeful availment of the benefits of the forum state. (Photo, Inc. v. Brand Technologies., Inc. (9th Cir. 2011) 647 F.3d 1218, 1229.)

For the responses to which defendants are claiming a privilege, defendants shall produce a privilege log. Responsive documents and privilege log shall be produced by June 6, 2025. Defendants shall provide information regarding custodians, non-custodial sources, and search terms by May 19, 2025.

To the extent any issues remain that were raised by this motion, the parties may file supplemental briefs of no more than five pages by June 25, 2025, regarding any outstanding issues.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling as modified.
